context: The mid-1950s was the height of Germany's Wirtschaftswunder (economic miracle), and music reflected optimism and escapism. German Schlager dominated with cheerful, sentimental songs, while American and international hits also charted strongly. Caterina Valente emerged as the superstar of 1955, with her French-themed hit topping the chart for five consecutive months.
